message:
fix(func): handle sign() edge cases for NaN/Infinity and numeric overflow

Three fixes to sign():

1. Float arm: NaN now returns NULL instead of 0. C SQLite returns NULL
   for sign(0.0/0.0) but the > / < comparisons both fail for NaN,
   incorrectly falling through to the else branch returning 0.

2. Text arm: Replace blanket is_nan()/is_infinite() rejection with
   keyword-level detection. Only reject literal "nan"/"inf"/"infinity"
   strings (case-insensitive, with optional leading sign). Numeric
   strings that overflow to infinity like "1e999" are valid — C SQLite's
   sqlite3AtoF parses them and sign() returns 1/-1.

3. Added tests for all edge cases: Float NaN, literal nan/inf keywords
   with signs, numeric overflow "1e999"/"-1e999", underflow "1e-999".

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
